edges.source: CTLA-4 Checkpoint Failure in Regulatory T Cells
CTLA-4 Checkpoint Failure in Regulatory T Cells

edges.description: Loss of CTLA-4-mediated immune restraint permits autoreactive effector responses.
Checkpoint failure can drive autoimmune cytopenias targeting platelets.
Checkpoint failure can drive autoimmune cytopenias targeting erythrocytes.
Lymphocyte accumulation in lymphoid tissue produces pathologic lymph node enlargement.
Tissue-directed lymphocytic inflammation can involve the gastrointestinal tract.
Loss of circulating and memory B-cell compartments reduces antibody output, producing a CVID-like hypogammaglobulinemia phenotype.

edges.target: CTLA-4 Checkpoint Failure in Regulatory T Cells

nodes.description: Abatacept is a CTLA-4-Ig fusion protein that functionally replaces the lost checkpoint signal by blocking CD80/CD86-mediated co-stimulation. It is the prototypic targeted therapy for CTLA4 haploinsufficiency and can induce rapid improvement in refractory immune dysregulation, especially enteropathy, arthritis, and lymphoproliferative disease.

evidence.reference_title: Abatacept for treatment-refractory pediatric CTLA4-haploinsufficiency.
evidence.snippet: Here we present two pediatric cases of severe CTLA4-haploinsufficiency refractory to conventional immunosuppressive therapies that responded rapidly to treatment with abatacept.

evidence.reference_title: Cytotoxic T Lymphocyte Antigen 4 Haploinsufficiency Presenting As Refractory Celiac-Like Disease: Case Report.
evidence.snippet: They are both now in complete clinical and endoscopic remission on abatacept.

nodes.description: Enteropathy may present as chronic diarrhea, celiac-like disease, or very early onset inflammatory bowel disease and can be severe enough to require targeted checkpoint replacement therapy.

evidence.reference_title: CTLA-4 Haploinsufficiency Presenting as Extensive Enteropathy in a Patient With Very Early Onset Inflammatory Bowel Disease.
evidence.snippet: We discuss a case of VEO-IBD in which the patient presented with severe and refractory enteropathy, leading to diagnosis of CTLA-4 haploinsufficiency.

evidence.reference_title: Cytotoxic T Lymphocyte Antigen 4 Haploinsufficiency Presenting As Refractory Celiac-Like Disease: Case Report.
evidence.snippet: Primary immunodeficiency may present with treatment-refractory enteropathy.

nodes.description: Autoimmune hemolytic anemia is a recurrent cytopenic manifestation and may occur alongside thrombocytopenia or broader Evans syndrome-like disease.

evidence.reference_title: Immune dysregulation in human subjects with heterozygous germline mutations in CTLA4.
evidence.snippet: Patient B.I.1, previously diagnosed with common variable immunodeficiency (CVID), had hepatosplenomegaly, autoimmune hemolytic anemia (AIHA), autoimmune thrombocytopenia, pulmonary nodules, and cerebral infiltrative lesions.

nodes.description: Immune-mediated platelet destruction is a frequent autoimmune cytopenia in affected individuals.

evidence.reference_title: Immune dysregulation in human subjects with heterozygous germline mutations in CTLA4.
evidence.snippet: Our index patient—a 22-year-old female (A.II.1)—developed brain, gastrointestinal (GI), and lung lymphocytic infiltrates, autoimmune thrombocytopenia, and hypogammaglobulinemia in early childhood.

nodes.description: Autoimmune disease is central to the syndrome and may affect the hematologic, gastrointestinal, dermatologic, endocrine, hepatic, or neurologic systems.

evidence.reference_title: Autosomal dominant immune dysregulation syndrome in humans with CTLA4 mutations.
evidence.snippet: We studied a large family in which five individuals presented with a complex, autosomal dominant immune dysregulation syndrome characterized by hypogammaglobulinemia, recurrent infections and multiple autoimmune clinical features.

nodes.description: CTLA4 haploinsufficiency destabilizes the B-cell compartment, causing progressive loss of circulating B cells, contraction of switched-memory B cells, and expansion of autoreactive CD21low B cells.

evidence.reference_title: Immune dysregulation in human subjects with heterozygous germline mutations in CTLA4.
evidence.snippet: Patients also exhibited progressive loss of circulating B cells, associated with an increase of predominantly autoreactive CD21(lo) B cells and accumulation of B cells in nonlymphoid organs.

evidence.reference_title: Autosomal dominant immune dysregulation syndrome in humans with CTLA4 mutations.
evidence.snippet: Mutations in CTLA4 were also associated with decreased circulating B cell numbers.

nodes.description: CTLA-4 is a core inhibitory checkpoint used by regulatory T cells to restrain CD28-mediated activation of conventional T cells. Heterozygous CTLA4 mutations lower CTLA-4 abundance or disrupt ligand binding, impairing Treg suppressive function and CD80 transendocytosis. This creates a primary defect in peripheral tolerance that propagates downstream immune dysregulation.
nodes.id: CTLA-4 Checkpoint Failure in Regulatory T Cells

biological_processes: negative regulation of T cell activation

evidence.reference_title: Immune dysregulation in human subjects with heterozygous germline mutations in CTLA4.
evidence.snippet: human CTLA4 haploinsufficiency caused dysregulation of FoxP3(+) regulatory T (Treg) cells, hyperactivation of effector T cells, and lymphocytic infiltration of target organs.

evidence.reference_title: Autosomal dominant immune dysregulation syndrome in humans with CTLA4 mutations.
evidence.snippet: However, CTLA-4 protein expression was decreased in regulatory T cells (Treg cells) in both patients and carriers with CTLA4 mutations. Whereas Treg cells were generally present at elevated numbers in these individuals, their suppressive function, CTLA-4 ligand binding and transendocytosis of CD80 were impaired.

evidence.reference_title: Autosomal dominant immune dysregulation syndrome in humans with CTLA4 mutations.
evidence.snippet: We identified a heterozygous nonsense mutation in exon 1 of CTLA4. Screening of 71 unrelated patients with comparable clinical phenotypes identified five additional families (nine individuals) with previously undescribed splice site and missense mutations in CTLA4.

evidence.reference_title: Preponderance of CTLA4 Variation Associated With Autosomal Dominant Immune Dysregulation in the MYPPPY Motif.
evidence.snippet: This variant lay within the highly conserved MYPPPY motif of CTLA-4: a critical structural determinant of ligand binding, which is also bound by the anti-CTLA-4 monoclonal antibody ipilimumab.

evidence.reference_title: Immune dysregulation in human subjects with heterozygous germline mutations in CTLA4.
evidence.snippet: We identified germline heterozygous mutations in CTLA4 in subjects with severe immune dysregulation from four unrelated families.

evidence.reference_title: CTLA4 / autoimmune lymphoproliferative syndrome due to CTLA4 haploinsufficiency (Definitive)
evidence.snippet: CTLA4 | HGNC:2505 | autoimmune lymphoproliferative syndrome due to CTLA4 haploinsufficiency | MONDO:0014493 | AD | Definitive

nodes.description: When CTLA-4-mediated restraint is reduced, conventional effector T cells become pathologically activated and sustain excess co-stimulatory signaling and inflammatory cytokine output.

evidence.reference_title: Immune dysregulation in human subjects with heterozygous germline mutations in CTLA4.
evidence.snippet: Our data indicate that germline CTLA4 haploinsufficiency causes lymphoproliferation, lymphocytic infiltration of nonlymphoid organs, autoimmune cytopenias, and B cell abnormalities with an accumulation of CD21lo B cells.

nodes.description: Reduced serum immunoglobulin concentrations are a common manifestation and often contribute to a CVID-like clinical presentation with recurrent infections.

evidence.reference_title: Autosomal dominant immune dysregulation syndrome in humans with CTLA4 mutations.
evidence.snippet: We studied a large family in which five individuals presented with a complex, autosomal dominant immune dysregulation syndrome characterized by hypogammaglobulinemia, recurrent infections and multiple autoimmune clinical features.

nodes.description: Pathologic lymph node enlargement reflects unchecked lymphocyte activation and tissue accumulation.

evidence.reference_title: Autosomal dominant immune dysregulation syndrome in humans with CTLA4 mutations.
evidence.snippet: Lymphadenopathy (Fig. 2e) and hepatosplenomegaly were also found in patients (Supplementary Notes).

nodes.description: Hyperactivated lymphocytes accumulate in lymphoid and nonlymphoid tissues, producing lymphoproliferation and inflammatory infiltration of organs including the gut, lung, bone marrow, and central nervous system.

evidence.reference_title: Immune dysregulation in human subjects with heterozygous germline mutations in CTLA4.
evidence.snippet: Our data indicate that germline CTLA4 haploinsufficiency causes lymphoproliferation, lymphocytic infiltration of nonlymphoid organs, autoimmune cytopenias, and B cell abnormalities with an accumulation of CD21lo B cells.

evidence.reference_title: Immune dysregulation in human subjects with heterozygous germline mutations in CTLA4.
evidence.snippet: Our index patient—a 22-year-old female (A.II.1)—developed brain, gastrointestinal (GI), and lung lymphocytic infiltrates, autoimmune thrombocytopenia, and hypogammaglobulinemia in early childhood.
